query: Terrion Arnold Detroit Lions Commissioner's Exempt List released waived NFL transaction status July 2026
answer:
## Research Complete

**Terrion Arnold's status (as of July 9, 2026): Unrestricted free agent — NOT on the Commissioner's Exempt List.**

Key findings:

- **He was released/waived by the Detroit Lions** (announced ~late June/early July 2026, officially reported the following Wednesday) amid **eight felony charges** in Florida stemming from a February 2026 armed robbery and kidnapping incident.
- **Waiver claiming deadline:** Monday, July 6, 2026, at 4:00 p.m. ET.
- **He cleared waivers unclaimed on July 6, 2026**, becoming a free agent. Despite reported interest from at least **3 NFL teams**, none claimed him — attributed to the **two years remaining** on his first-round rookie contract plus the legal situation.
- **He is NOT on the Commissioner's Exempt List.** That list (paid leave) applies only to rostered players; because the Lions released him rather than retaining him, the Exempt List mechanism never applied. Reporting noted he *could* have been placed on paid leave *only if* he had stayed on a roster.
- He posted a **$1 million bond** with restrictive conditions (home/work confinement, no witness contact, passport forfeiture).

The question's framing conflates two alternative outcomes: the Lions chose the **waive/release** path (→ cleared waivers → free agent), not the **Commissioner's Exempt List** path.

sources.snippet: The Lions announced the release on Monday; the official move appears on Wednesday's NFL transactions report. Claiming deadline is Monday, July 6, at 4:00 p.m. ET. He would possibly be placed on paid leave by the NFL if he were on a roster.

sources.title: Why the Detroit Lions cut Terrion Arnold now
sources.snippet: Hours after a Florida judge set Terrion Arnold's bond at $1 million, the Lions cut ties with the 23-year-old cornerback.

key_facts: Detroit Lions released/waived CB Terrion Arnold in late June/early July 2026 amid felony criminal charges; the release was announced on a Monday and officially appeared on the following Wednesday's NFL transaction report.
Arnold is NOT on the NFL Commissioner's Exempt List; he was waived and cleared waivers, becoming a free agent rather than being placed on the Exempt List.
Waiver claiming deadline was Monday, July 6, 2026 at 4:00 p.m. ET.
Arnold cleared waivers unclaimed on July 6, 2026 (reported by NFL Network's Mike Garafolo), making him an unrestricted free agent.
No team claimed him despite reported interest from at least 3 NFL teams, largely due to the two years remaining on his first-round rookie contract and pending charges.
Arnold faces eight (8) felony charges in Florida from a February 2026 armed robbery and kidnapping incident.
He posted a $1 million bond with conditions including home/work restriction, no contact with co-defendants/witnesses, and passport forfeiture.
The Commissioner's Exempt List (paid leave) could have applied only if he had remained on an NFL roster; because he was released, that mechanism did not apply.
Arnold was the Lions' 2024 first-round draft pick (former Alabama Crimson Tide star), a 23-year-old cornerback.
